name: Drever
description: The Drever is Sweden's most common hunting breed by numbers, a short-legged German-derived scent hound used to hunt deer, fox, and hare in Scandinavian forests. Developed from the Westphalian Dachsbracke imported to Sweden in the early 20th century and crossed with local hounds, the Drever was officially recognized as a Swedish breed in 1947 after popular demand from hunters. Its short legs allow it to move through dense undergrowth where taller hounds would struggle, while its loud voice carries well in the deep forests of Scandinavia.

conditions_detail.name: Hip Dysplasia
conditions_detail.description: Hip dysplasia is a genetic condition where the hip joint doesn't develop properly, causing the ball and socket to not fit together correctly. This leads to joint deterioration, pain, and arthritis over time.
conditions_detail.treatment_overview: Treatment ranges from weight management and physical therapy for mild cases to total hip replacement for severe cases. NSAIDs and joint supplements help manage pain.
prevention_tips: Screen breeding stock with hip scoring
